# Transcrição: 09-Athiest in a Hot Dog Line

- **Fonte:** 09-Athiest in a Hot Dog Line
- **Duração:** 07:32
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Thank you. I fell in love with God when I was seven years old. My grandparents gave me a nativity scene. It was a good one. Real wood. I had all the figures. I had three crooked and posing wise men, an utterly befuddled Joseph. And even though Mary was cast in plasticine, you could tell she was pure as the driven snow. They were my first action figures, and I loved them. My parents took the nativity scene, and they put it beside the family Bible, this big gold leafed book that they said I could not touch because it was the Word of God. And so I played with the baby Jesus next to the Word of God, and that is where I found my faith. And it was real for me at that age. I talked to God all the time, so much so that a child psychologist was called in to find out if he was speaking back to me when they found out I wasn't hearing anything, they let me go. But it was important to me at that age, because I was growing up in a broken home with absentee parents, and I just felt alone so often as a kid. But knowing that God was there and I could just talk to an adult figure and talk about my problems, it meant everything to me. And so when I turned about 10, my parents made a terrible decision that changed my life forever. They sent me to Sunday school. I found myself in a classroom on the second floor of the church listening to a nice lady tell a story about Noah and the Ark, a story I had never heard before. And when she finished the story, I raised my hand and I said, where does that story come from? And she said the Bible, the Word of God. And I couldn't believe it, because even at 10, I knew that story was bullshit. I knew that polar bears from the North Pole and penguins from the South Pole did not converge in the middle and get aboard a boat. I knew that 40 days under the water was going to kill every tree on the planet. And I obsessed over the kittens. How many kittens did God drown to make a point? That blanket of faith that I had held so close to me felt thin and tattered. And so the next week, I'm back in the Sunday school and she's reading a story again, and this time it's Jonah and the whale. Three pages in, I knew that story was bullshit too. And when she told me that one came from the Bible too, for the first time in a long time I felt the ground underneath me unsteady. And I didn't want to go back the third week, but I did. And it was the week they were telling the story of Adam and Eve, and I knew that one

## [03:00 - 06:04]

and I was okay with it. Even the talking snake, like the devil has some serious shit, he can make snakes talk, I can live with that. But when the story was over, there was this unanswered question. So I raised my hand and she hated me by now. But I said, what happens next? Like, do Adam and Eve's kids have kids? Or do they have kids with their kids? Like, how does it keep going? And she was pissed. But I also saw in her face confusion. Like, she had never considered the question before. And that was it. Truly, that was the moment my faith fell away. That blanket that kept me warm, I left it behind. I stopped talking to God on that day. And for the next 30 years truly, I fought for faith. I went to church after church after church, looking for a place that would make me feel the way I did as a kid. I went to temples and synagogues and mosques. I tried to find God in more places than you can imagine and I just couldn't. And then I got married to Alicia, my perfect wife, and we had two children, Clara and Charlie. And every night before we go to bed, we get the kids on our bed and we read to them and then we turn off the lights and we play music before sending them away. And it's the best part of my day, the best part of every day. It is not an exaggeration to say that when I sit on that bed in the dark, there isn't a moment every single night when I think about how fleeting this family is and how our love that is so strong on that bed someday will be no more. In a not too distant future, each one of us is going to die and there is no place for us after this. It's dark, I know. Every night it is that for me. And then five years ago, something changed. It was Father's Day. I was at the new Britain Rock Cats Minor League Baseball game and I'm in a very long hot dog line. And when I'm waiting like this, I like to be productive. I'm a joy to be around. And I've got to send an email. There's a guy a hundred miles north at a YMCA camp. I take my students, I'm a teacher every year to this YMCA camp and in the summer we make plans and this guy, Pat, has not been responding to my emails and he's making me crazy so I decide here's where I'm going to email him. Again in this line, answer me, Pat. And I open up my email client and I open up the email and I type his name and I look up and Pat is standing in front of me. Now it's not Pat, it can't be Pat because that is impossible. It is a guy that looks like Pat, a guy I've only met three times in my life and lives a hundred miles away. He's cutting through the line right in front of me. He's leading the hot dog, the ketchup and the relish, the guys in the big costumes that run around the field in between innings and he's got a new Britain Rockcatch jersey on.

## [06:04 - 07:32]

And as he passes by me, I see the back of his uniform and it says Pat. I can't believe it. It's Pat. It's not possible. And so I run to my wife in the stands with my kids and I tell her what happened. I say, how can I email a guy who I barely know and suddenly he's in front of me when his name appears and she says that is the most amazing coincidence I've ever heard. And I say, what if it's not? What if it's God? What if that was the thing I've been waiting for? It's not enough. Moses gets a burning bush and the Red Sea parts right in front of him. I'm not impressed by his faith. Noah gets the most ridiculous weather forecast of all time and it happens. Of course they believe in God. I get an email in a hot dog line and Pat. It's incredible. I will never forget it, but it's not enough. But what I hope is that if there is a God and I hope there is, I hope that someday if I meet him or her, that wanting faith as much as anyone I have met in my life, that the wanting for faith will be enough. Thank you.
